subject: grammar
title: Exercise — Past Simple / Past Continuous
instruction: Fill in the gaps with the past simple or past continuous form of the verbs in brackets. In which gaps could you use *used to*?
passage: I 1 [1] (have) a wonderful biology teacher, Mrs Hughes. She 2 [2] (make) us excited about the subject because she was so interested herself. I remember one lesson in particular; we 3 [3] (study) different types of plants, and Mrs Hughes 4 [4] (describe) the different parts of the flower. She 5 [5] (pick up) a purple flower, I can't remember exactly what it was, and then suddenly we 6 [6] (notice) that she 7 [7] (cry)! She 8 [8] (apologise) and 9 [9] (say) that sometimes nature was so beautiful it just made her cry! We 10 [10] (not/know) what to do at first, but it certainly 11 [11] (make) us think. Something similar 12 [12] (happen) while she 13 [13] (show) us how to work the microscope. She 14 [14] (examine) a slide of some plant tissue and she 15 [15] (smile) all over her face. She suddenly 16 [16] (get) all excited and 17 [17] (say), 'Isn't it wonderful?' Some students 18 [18] (laugh) at her when she 19 [19] (not/look) but I didn't. Somehow her enthusiasm 20 [20] (inspire) me, and I 21 [21] (start) to like biology.

questions.text: I ___ (have) a wonderful biology teacher, Mrs Hughes.
She ___ (make) us excited about the subject because she was so interested herself.
I remember one lesson in particular; we ___ (study) different types of plants,
...and Mrs Hughes ___ (describe) the different parts of the flower.
She ___ (pick up) a purple flower, I can't remember exactly what it was,
...and then suddenly we ___ (notice) that she was crying!
...we noticed that she ___ (cry)!
She ___ (apologise)
...and ___ (say) that sometimes nature was so beautiful it just made her cry!
We ___ (not/know) what to do at first,
...but it certainly ___ (make) us think.
Something similar ___ (happen) while she was showing us how to work the microscope.
Something similar happened while she ___ (show) us how to work the microscope.
She ___ (examine) a slide of some plant tissue
...and she ___ (smile) all over her face.
She suddenly ___ (get) all excited
...and ___ (say), 'Isn't it wonderful?'
Some students ___ (laugh) at her when she wasn't looking
Some students laughed at her when she ___ (not/look)
Somehow her enthusiasm ___ (inspire) me,
...and I ___ (start) to like biology.
